categories.name: What grasslands do for the climate

fun_fact: When most people think of India's wildlife, they picture dense tropical jungles or dry deciduous forests. But India's grasslands are unsung ecological powerhouses. Covering roughly 20% of the country's geographical area, they range from freezing alpine meadows in the Himalayas to scorched, semi-arid plains in the west. Far from being just "empty spaces," these ecosystems support some of the rarest wildlife on Earth and sustain millions of pastoral communities. [Read more](https://india.mongabay.com/2025/08/asias-largest-grassland-is-a-surprisingly-effective-carbon-sink-study-finds/)
